title: hieracium dasytrichum subsp. subpiliferum
intro: Hieracium dasytrichum subsp. subpiliferum, also known as the Hawkweed, is a rare and lesser-known species of flowering plant in the dandelion family. The plant's leaves, flowers, and roots have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, particularly to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hieracium dasytrichum subsp. subpiliferum include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
